title: Unit 29: Diminutives and Augmentatives - Grammar Podcast

script.text: Unit 29 focuses on the emotional texture of Modern Greek: Diminutives and Augmentatives. These suffixes allow speakers to express size, affection, or familiarity. Athina, what is the universal 'tiny-maker'?
Είναι το '-άκι'. Μετατρέπει τα πάντα σε ουδέτερα. Το σπίτι - το σπιτάκι, ο καφές - το καφεδάκι.
Correct. This suffix is not just for size, but for politeness. However, we also have gender-specific diminutives, such as '-itsa' and '-oula' for feminine nouns. Athina?
Η μπύρα - η μπιρίτσα, η αδερφή - η αδερφούλα. Ακούγεται πολύ πιο φιλικό.
In contrast, augmentatives like '-aros' and '-ara' are utilized to express enormous size or admiration. Athina, demonstrate.
Το σκυλί - ο σκύλαρος, το αυτοκίνητο - η αυτοκινητάρα. Είναι κάτι τεράστιο!

Exactly. Lastly, we must observe how diminutives minimize imposition in requests or offers. This concludes our academic overview of Unit 29. Mastering these suffixes is essential for achieving a nuanced, native-like tone in Modern Greek.
